name: Qiskit (IBM Quantum)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); IBM Quantum access free (limited); premium plans for more QPU time

auth.notes: IBM Quantum API token for hardware access; local simulation no auth

performance.notes: Local simulation fast; IBM hardware queue can be minutes to hours

agent_readiness.notes: IBM's quantum computing SDK. Circuit building, transpilation, execution. Multiple backends: local simulators (Aer), IBM Quantum hardware (127-433 qubits), cloud simulators. Runtime Primitives API (Sampler, Estimator). Error mitigation. Pulse-level control. Qiskit Ecosystem: Machine Learning, Nature, Finance, Optimization packages. The most widely deployed quantum SDK — largest user community and hardware access.

gotchas: IBM hardware access limited without paid plan
Hardware noise makes results probabilistic and unreliable for near-term
Significant API changes between versions (Qiskit 1.x broke Qiskit 0.x)
Quantum circuit design requires quantum computing knowledge
Practical quantum advantage rare for current NISQ hardware
best_when: IBM Quantum hardware access (largest public quantum fleet)
Teams needing most mature and documented quantum SDK
avoid_when: Production computation (NISQ hardware too noisy for most tasks)
When PennyLane's QML integration is needed
summary: Qiskit is IBM's quantum computing SDK — the most widely used and documented quantum SDK. Circuit building, transpilation, local simulation (Aer), and IBM hardware execution. Runtime Primitives API with error mitigation. Qiskit Ecosystem for ML, chemistry, and optimization. Best for quantum research with access to IBM's hardware fleet. NISQ hardware too noisy for most practical applications.
